element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Baseball bat melee attack established before entering Dark World
Fusion mechanic explained; transformation power introduced before gameplay
Primary attack method used throughout all levels
Required for boss defeats; many bosses necessitate rebounding projectiles back at them
Dropped by enemies; establishes risk/reward of engagement
Grants flight and beam attacks; limited duration; power gradually improves through level completion
Provides randomized rewards (health/points/transformation); adds uncertainty to navigation
Accessed via doorways in main level; key obtained by defeating bosses
Earned by defeating bosses; required to progress to next level
Many doors lead to empty rooms; adds navigation challenge and uncertainty
8-character passwords allow access to any of 8 levels without starting over
Bosses escalate in absurdity; include Mummy, Spiders, Zombies, Medusa, Dragon, Grim Reaper, and surreal entities (giant fried shrimp, talking caterpillar, walking pants, giant cat hurling kittens)

typical_tension_triggers: Opening unknown doors (potential empty room or boss encounter)
Health depletion from sustained boss engagement
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Episodic tension structure: each door represents a decision point and potential encounter; boss fights create extended tension peaks; random question-mark rewards provide small relief moments within level traversal

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Doorways in main level area; many lead to nothing, forcing player to systematically explore and risk unknown encounters
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Question mark items appear from all doors regardless of encounter; potential items hidden behind exploration of dead-ends

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics (bat attacks, projectile deflection, Bert transformation); early bosses feature modified parody designs intended to be less graphically intense than Japanese prototype
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating boss complexity; increased reliance on projectile deflection mastery; Bert transformation power gradually strengthens
difficulty_curve.late_game: Surreal and unconventional boss designs (fried shrimp that transforms into onion ring then shish kebab); sustained encounter difficulty
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (plant boss) - introduces projectile deflection requirement
Level progression through escalating horror parodies
Final encounter with Dark World Master

notable_level_patterns.description: Main level is side-scrolling traversal space; doorways provide access to isolated boss chambers; many doors lead to empty rooms. Completing door entry grants question-mark item regardless of encounter.
Multiple bosses require rebounding projectiles back at them rather than direct attacks; Mark's bat has limited offensive capability; necessitates mastery of deflection mechanic
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Giant fried shrimp boss (transforms through multiple food forms)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level features thematic horror parodies progressing through classic movie references (censored in US version), Japanese folklore entities, and original absurdist designs
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mummy, Medusa, Dragon, Grim Reaper as traditional encounters; talking caterpillar, bouncing fried shrimp as novel designs

notable_level_patterns.description: Transformation pills grant flight and beam attacks; beam effect strength gradually improves with level completion, providing progression feedback
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Available in all levels; power increases from Level 1 through Level 8

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Monster Party)
